name: isBrowser
description: A boolean constant that accurately determines if the code is currently running in a browser environment.
usage_guidelines: Use this for preventing SSR errors when accessing the `window` or `document` objects.
Better than just `typeof window !== 'undefined'` as it checks for `window.document.createElement`.

return_value.description: True if executing in a browser; false otherwise (SSR).

Do: Use it in Next.js Server/Client component barriers where window might not exist.
Dont: Do not wrap entire components inside isBrowser condition instead of standard Next.js dynamic imports.
